academy.name: TheLearningHyena Spanish Academy
academy.description: Gamified Spanish learning platform combining 3D interactive spirit companions, structured workbook lessons, daily quests, card collectibles, and active immersion.

features.basicEspanolPart1: Core curriculum Part 1: Lesson 1 (Pronunciation & Greetings), Lesson 2 (Subject Pronouns), Lesson 3 (Nouns & Definite Articles), Lesson 4 (Everyday Sentences), and Part 1 Workbook Exam (50 coins).
features.basicEspanolPart2: Core curriculum Part 2: Lesson 5 (Indefinite Articles & Numbers 0-100), Lesson 6 (Verb Estar & Numbers >100), Lesson 7 (Regular -er/-ir Verbs in Present), Lesson 8 (Verb Ir & Question Words), and Part 2 Master Exam (50 coins + badge).
features.basicEspanolPart3: Core curriculum Part 3: Lesson 9 (Expressing Time & Calendar), Lesson 10 (Expressions Using Tener), Lesson 11 (Verbs like Hacer & Weather), Lesson 12 (Saber vs Conocer), and Part 3 Workbook Exam.
features.basicEspanolPart4: Core curriculum Part 4: Lesson 13 (Stem-Changing Verbs e->ie/o->ue/e->i), Lesson 14 (Ver, Dar & Yo-Go Verbs), Lesson 15 (Present Progressive), Lesson 16 (Direct Object Pronouns & Adverbs), and Part 4 Master Exam.
features.basicEspanolPart5: Core curriculum Part 5: Lesson 17 (Affirmatives, Negatives & Demonstratives), Lesson 18 (More Affirmative & Negative Expressions), Lesson 19 (Indirect Object Pronouns & Gustar), Lesson 20 (Double Object Pronouns me lo / se lo), and Part 5 Master Exam.
features.basicEspanolPart6: Core curriculum Part 6: Lesson 21 (Reflexive Verbs & Daily Routine), Lesson 22 (Talking about Past: Acabar & Hace), Lesson 23 (Present Duration: ¿Desde Cuándo...?), Lesson 24 (Formal Commands & Unequal Comparisons), Lesson 25 (Informal Commands Tú haz/ten/pon/sal/ve/di/ven/sé), and Part 6 Master Exam.
features.basicEspanolPart7: Core curriculum Part 7: Lesson 26 (Superlatives & Equal Comparisons), Lesson 27 (Regular -ar Verbs in Preterite), Lesson 28 (Regular -er/-ir Verbs in Preterite), Lesson 29 (Irregular Verbs in Preterite), Lesson 30 (Preterite vs Imperfect & Next Steps in Spanish Mastery), and Part 7 Grand Master Exam.
features.dailyQuests: Daily tasks (e.g. practicing speaking, completing lessons, maintaining streak) that grant Gold Coins and XP.
features.shop: Unlock legendary collectible cards from One Piece and Demon Slayer series using earned Gold Coins.
features.adventureMap: Explore 7 world nodes (Pueblo Inicial, Bosque de Verbos, El Reloj del Tiempo, El Mercado del Progreso, La Ciudad del Pronombre, La Rutina Diaria y Comandos, El Templo del Pasado), fight 21 boss encounters, and claim all 9 Kitsune Tails.
features.activeImmersion: Practice real-world roleplay scenarios, daily plans, and interactive conversation practice.

grammarTips.serVsEstar: SER is for permanent traits (DOCTOR: Description, Occupation, Characteristic, Time, Origin, Relation). ESTAR is for temporary states/locations (PLACE: Position, Location, Action, Condition, Emotion, Health). Conjugations of ESTAR: estoy, estás, está, estamos, estáis, están.
grammarTips.verbIr: IR means 'to go' (voy, vas, va, vamos, vais, van). Near future formula: IR + A + INFINITIVE (e.g. 'voy a estudiar' = I am going to study).
grammarTips.erAndIrVerbs: Present tense endings for -ER verbs (-o, -es, -e, -emos, -éis, -en) and -IR verbs (-o, -es, -e, -imos, -ís, -en). Common verbs: comer (to eat), beber (to drink), vivir (to live), escribir (to write), aprender (to learn).
grammarTips.numbers: Numbers 0-100 (uno, diez, veinte, treinta, cuarenta...) and 100+ (cien, ciento, doscientos, quinientos, mil, un millón).
grammarTips.greetings: Buenos días (Good morning), Buenas tardes (Good afternoon), Buenas noches (Good evening), ¿Cómo estás? (How are you?).
grammarTips.pronouns: Yo (I), Tú (you informal), Él/Ella (he/she), Nosotros (we), Vosotros (you all Spain), Ellos/Ellas (they).
grammarTips.datesAndTime: Time uses SER: 'Es la una' (1:00) / 'Son las dos' (2:00). Days of the week (lunes, martes...) use 'el'. Months (enero, febrero...) and seasons (primavera, verano, otoño, invierno).
grammarTips.tenerExpressions: Idiomatic expressions using TENER: tener hambre (to be hungry), tener sed (to be thirsty), tener frío (to be cold), tener calor (to be hot), tener prisa (to be in a hurry), tener miedo (to be afraid), tener años (to be X years old).
grammarTips.hacerAndWeather: Weather expressions use HACER: hace frío (it is cold), hace calor (it is hot), hace sol (it is sunny), hace buen tiempo (good weather). Also used for tasks: hacer la tarea (do homework), hacer la cama (make bed).
grammarTips.saberVsConocer: SABER: facts, information, learned skills (sé, sabes, sabe...; saber + infinitive). CONOCER: people, places, familiarity (conozco, conoces, conoce...; conocer + a + persona).
grammarTips.stemChangingVerbs: Boot verbs change vowel in present (yo, tú, él, ellos): e->ie (querer -> quiero, pensar -> pienso), o->ue (poder -> puedo, dormir -> duermo), e->i (pedir -> pido, servir -> sirvo). Nosotros and vosotros do NOT change stem.
grammarTips.yoGoVerbs: Verbs with irregular 'yo' form ending in -go: pongo (poner), salgo (salir), traigo (traer), hago (hacer), tengo (tener), vengo (venir).
grammarTips.presentProgressive: Formed with ESTAR + Gerund (-ando for -AR, -iendo for -ER/-IR). Example: estoy hablando (I am talking), están comiendo (they are eating), estamos viviendo (we are living).
grammarTips.directObjectPronouns: Replaces the direct object receiver: me, te, lo (him/it), la (her/it), nos, os, los (them m.), las (them f.). Position: before conjugated verb or attached to infinitive/gerund.
grammarTips.demonstratives: Demonstrative adjectives and pronouns by distance: Este/esta/estos/estas (this/these - close), Ese/esa/esos/esas (that/those - medium), Aquel/aquella/aquellos/aquellas (that/those over there - far).
grammarTips.affirmativesAndNegatives: Pairs: algo (something) / nada (nothing), alguien (someone) / nadie (nobody), siempre (always) / nunca or jamás (never), también (also) / tampoco (neither), alguno (some) / ninguno (none). Spanish allows double negatives: 'No tengo nada'.
grammarTips.indirectObjectPronounsAndGustar: Indirect pronouns: me, te, le, nos, os, les. Used with GUSTAR-type verbs (gustar, encantar, interesar, faltar, molestar). Structure: [Indirect Pronoun] + [Gusta/Gustan] + [Subject]. Example: 'Me gustan las flores'.
grammarTips.doubleObjectPronouns: Using Indirect + Direct together: Indirect comes FIRST (People before Things). When 'le/les' precedes 'lo/la/los/las', change 'le/les' to SE. Formula: me lo, te lo, se lo, nos lo, os lo, se lo. Example: 'Se lo doy' (I give it to him).
grammarTips.reflexiveVerbs: Action done to oneself: me, te, se, nos, os, se. Daily routines: lavarse (me lavo), levantarse (me levanto), vestirse (me visto e->i), acostarse (me acuesto o->ue), dormirse (me duermo).
grammarTips.acabarAndHace: Acabar de + Infinitive = recent past ('Acabo de comer' = I have just eaten). Hace + time + que = duration of past action ('Hace dos años que estudio').
grammarTips.formalAndInformalCommands: Formal Commands (Usted/Ustedes): swap vowel endings (-AR -> -e/-en, -ER/-IR -> -a/-an; e.g. Hable, Coma). Informal Commands (Tú affirmative): use 3rd person singular present (Habla, Come). Irregular Tú commands: Haz (hacer), Ten (tener), Pon (poner), Sal (salir), Ve (ir), Di (decir), Ven (venir), Sé (ser).
grammarTips.comparisonsAndSuperlatives: Unequal: más... que (more... than), menos... que (less... than). Equal: tan + adj + como (as... as), tanto/a/os/as + noun + como (as much/many... as). Superlative: el/la más... or suffix -ísimo/a (altísimo = extremely tall). Irregulars: mejor (better), peor (worse).
grammarTips.preteriteTense: Completed past action at a specific time. Regular -AR: -é, -aste, -ó, -amos, -asteis, -aron (hablé, habló). Regular -ER/-IR: -í, -iste, -ió, -imos, -isteis, -ieron (comí, comió, viví, vivió). Irregulars: Fui (ser/ir), Hice (hacer), Tuve (tener), Estuve (estar), Pude (poder), Dije (decir).
grammarTips.preteriteVsImperfect: PRETERITE: completed past event, specific start/end, interrupting action (What happened: 'Ayer hablé'). IMPERFECT: background description, ongoing past state, habitual action, age, time in past (What was happening / used to happen: 'Era niño', 'Llovía').
